_description: Happy path — planned laparoscopic inguinal hernia repair. Normal adult, full wallet, private Tier-1 hospital. All mandatory docs available. Should sail through all phases.

clinical.chief_complaints: Left groin swelling and dragging pain for 6 months, worsening on exertion, reducible on lying down, no bowel complaints
clinical.history_of_present_illness: Patient noticed a swelling in left groin 6 months ago, initially small and reducible. Swelling has gradually increased in size. Associated with dragging pain on exertion and prolonged standing. Swelling reduces on lying down. No history of vomiting, constipation, or urinary symptoms. No prior surgeries in this region.

examination_findings.general: Conscious, oriented, well-built, no pallor, no icterus
examination_findings.cvs: S1 S2 heard, no murmurs, pulse regular
examination_findings.rs: Clear air entry bilateral, no added sounds
examination_findings.abdomen: Soft, non-tender. Left inguinal swelling 5x4 cm, reducible, cough impulse positive, reaches inguinal ring
examination_findings.local: Left inguinal region: reducible swelling, positive cough impulse, does not transilluminate

investigations.result_summary: Left inguinal hernia confirmed — hernial sac with bowel loops, reducible, no obstruction
Pre-operative blood workup: Hb 13.8 g/dL, WBC 8200/cumm, platelets 2.4 lakh, creatinine 0.9 mg/dL, blood glucose fasting 98 mg/dL, PT/INR 1.0

clinical.past_medical_history: No significant past medical history. No prior hospitalisations.

non_clinical_documents_in_hand.label: Photo of patient on hospital bed

clinical.notes: Patient fit for surgery. Pre-anaesthetic check-up done and cleared. Planned for elective laparoscopic TEP repair under general anaesthesia.
